role_summary: SNCC field organizer; in this corpus's embedding his testimony clusters with Gwendolyn Zoharah Simmons, Harold K. Brown, and Charles McLaurin.
ai_reading: Churchville's top semantic neighbors are Gwendolyn Zoharah Simmons (entry 53, count 169), Harold K. Brown (entry 55, count 138, San Diego CORE), Myrtle Gonza Glascoe (entry 137), Charles McLaurin (entry 17, Mississippi SNCC), and James Oscar Jones (entry 57). The cluster bridges SNCC field organizing with West Coast civil rights leadership (Brown), reading him as a movement-network voice broader than a single-region or single-organization label captures. His position on the Southern Struggle vs Northern Struggle axis at -0.41 leans Northern, an unusual reading for a SNCC field organizer whose institutional placement is the Southern movement; the embedding may be catching post-1965 Northern Black Power organizing in his transcript over the Southern fieldwork.
biographical_paragraph: John Churchville's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 63 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
